title: The Destination of The Impersonal Meditator I Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa I SB 3.9.4 I 03.11.2022
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: OM NAMO BARVADEVASUDEVAYA
Reading from Srimad-Bharavatam, Tanto 3, Chapter 9, Brahma's Prayers for Creative Energy, verse number 4.
TADVAYIDAM BUVANAMANDALAMANDALAYA
GYANESHMANO DARSITAM TAD UPASATRANAM
TASMAI NAMO BARVATENU VIDEM ADUPYAM
YO NADRITO NARA TRABADRA VIRA SATPRASANDRAYA
Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shri.
Srila Prabhupada
This present form, or any transcendental form, expanded by the Supreme Personality of Godhead Sri Krishna,
is equally auspicious for all the universes.
Since you have manifested this eternal personal form upon whom your devotees meditate,
I therefore offer my respectful obeisances unto you,
those who are destined to be dispatched to the path of hell,
who are possessed by the selfless souls.
Do you not appreciate this form of yours?
Do you not have a perfect understanding of truth and reality?
Do you not believe that you are a material being?
Do you not have a perfect understanding of what the ultimate reality is,
of the eternal reality or the ultimate time?
Do you not have a perfect understanding of your own life or your own personality?
Since you have all these четtings,
you have no right to exercise this eternal life or know your own state of being.
If you wish to become a trader,
you must first demonstrate the true, eternal, and supreme truth of the Supreme Absolute Truth,
the eternal and eternal truth of the Supreme Absolute Truth in the spiritual world.
The personal form of the Lord is also worshipped in meditation as Supersoul , but the impersonal Brahmajyoti is not worshipped.
Persons who are addicted to the impersonal feature of the Lord, whether in meditation or otherwise, are all pilgrims to hell,
because, as stated in the Bhagavad Gita, impersonalists simply waste their time in mundane mental speculation,
because they are addicted more to false arguments than to reality.
Therefore, the association of impersonalists is condemned herewith by Brahma.
All the planar expansions of the personality of Godhead are equally potent as confirmed in Brahma Samhita
The Lord expands himself as the flames of a fire, expansing one after another.
Although the original flame, or Sri Krishna, is accepted as Govinda, the Supreme Person,
all other expansions such as Godhead, Simha, Brahma and even Sai Baba, can be accepted as Govinda.
Assumed by the Lord, the Lord is the true and supreme body of Godhead.
as Rama, Narasimha and Varaha are as potent as the original Lord. All such expanded forms
are transcendental. In the beginning of Srimad-Bhagavatam, it is made clear that the Supreme Truth is
eternally untaminated by material touch. There is no jugglery of words and activities in
the transcendental kingdom of the Lord. All the Lord's forms are transcendental and
such manifestations are ever-identical. The particular form of the Lord exhibited to
the devotee is not mundane, even though the devotee may retain material desire, nor is
it manifest under the influence of material energy, as it is foolishly considered by the
impersonalist. Impersonalists who consider the transcendental form of the Lord to be
products of the material world are surely destined for hell.
Thus ends the 받아 Veda and the purport.
